note: Two spacecraft leading edge measurement using triangulation to determine the longitude of the source; the CME becomes very diffuse with time and is extremely difficult to see in STA COR2 past 8 solar radii.

Best fit measurement of faint leading edge using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, C3 and STEREO A COR2. Strong eastern and southern deflection was visible from the source location seen in UV imagery of the Earth-facing disk, which is reflected in the best fit parameters.
Measurement using best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and two frames of STEREO A COR2.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO imagery and possible source location longitude.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A.
Measurement using best fit between several SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 frames. The leading edge becomes diffuse halfway through the STEREO A COR2 field of view.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, tracking well with the far southern latitude dimming around S60W20 observed in SDO AIA 193.

Best fit measurement of leading edge using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. Strong northern deflection was visible from the source location seen in UV imagery from SDO AIA, STEREO A EUVI, and GOES SUVI, which is reflected in the best fit parameters.

Measurement of the faint leading edge, based on source location seen in STEREO A EUVI and fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. No STEREO A COR2 imagery was available in real time for this CME due to a data gap which began at 2025-04-04T17:38Z. The leading edge becomes diffuse only a handful of frames into the SOHO LASCO C3 field of view.
Parameters based on best fit of features in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is some uncertainty due to lack of clear source in available EUV imagery.

Very approximate analysis of the main brightest front/loop, obtained by fitting with two coronagraphs. Based on the coronal signatures, the source is behind the east limb, but close enough to the limb for the tops of the post-eruptive arcades to start peaking from behind the limb two hours after the eruption.

A very approximate, speculative analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. The source is likely far behind the east limb and the front is not clear as the CME is largely overlapping with the earlier CME.
Analysis based solely on the fit with two coronagraphs, since the source is not completely clear and is likely behind the limb.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, and a few frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. The leading edge becomes diffuse partway through the SOHO LASCO C3 field of view.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. The source is behind the northeast limb.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. The source marked by post eruptive arcades seems to be just beyond the east limb.
Two spacecraft bulk measurement with high confidence in parameters, good fit relative to observed source location.
Shock measurement in SOHO and STEREO A by encompassing the faint northeast edge in both spacecraft as well as the bright bulk.
Leading edge measurement using two spacecraft to triangulate the longitude out to 14 solar radii.
Updated parameters based on best fit with more imagery.
Analysis based on the location of the source (filament eruption on disk) and confirmed by the fit in two coronagraphs. The longitude can vary a bit (+-5 degrees).

Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and confirmed by the approximate location of the likely source - filament off the northeast limb. The jet is seen only in a few timestamps of SOHO and STEREO because of its faintness, so this analysis is very approximate.

Measurement based on source location and using only C2 frames, since this CME has not yet reached the C3 field of view. No STEREO A COR2 imagery was available in real time for this CME due to a data gap which began at 2025-04-09T18:53Z.
Triangulated measurement with SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A imagery. LASCO C2 is not used due to the faintness of the front, it is better resolved in C3 and COR2A.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement of the faint leading edge using four frames of SOHO LASCO C2. Measurement was made using the candidate source longitude, taking into consideration the heavy southern deflection which was possibly indicated in UV imagery. The leading edge becomes diffuse before leaving the C2 field of view. No STEREO A COR2 imagery was available in real time for this CME due to a data gap which began at 2025-04-11T11:23Z.

Measurement based on source location and best fit using several frames of C2 and a few frames of C3. No STEREO A COR2 imagery was available in real time for this CME due to a data gap which began at 2025-04-11T11:23Z.

Measurement based on source location and best fit in several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and C3. No STEREO A COR2 imagery was available in real time for this CME due to a data gap which began at 2025-04-11T11:23Z.

Measurement using source location seen in UV imagery and several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and a few frames of C3. No STEREO A COR2 imagery was available in real time for this CME due to a data gap which began at 2025-04-11T11:23Z.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as source location. The CME appears to speed up in later frames. The CME may deflect to the east of the potential source location.
Best fit based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Best fit based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Best fit based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Best fit based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Best fit measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and on visible on-disk source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.
Shock measurement of the fainter feature traveling ahead of the bulk of the CME in the field of view in the coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, fits the area of highest/brightest post-eruptive arcades/dimming (S19E15), but with some southern deflection, it seems.
Measurement of leading edge using the candidate source location and the best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement of the southernmost lobes of the eruption. Measurement of leading edge using the candidate source location and the best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement of leading edge using the candidate source location and the best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement using a triangulated fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, two frames of SOHO LASCO C3, and a few frames of STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and confirmed by the coronal signatures (opening of field lines behind the southwest limb).
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Leading edge measurement with one spacecraft assuming the source location of the source is known; approximately 15 degree longitude uncertainty.
Leading edge measurement using two spacecraft to triangulate a potential far-sided longitude out to 7 solar radii.

Preliminary leading edge measurement using an isolated SOHO LASCO frame as well as STEREO A COR2 to triangulate the CME; the CME is likely faster than this measurement, but the longitude was largely obtained with this measurement.
Leading edge measurement using two spacecraft to triangulate a potential longitude; seen just beyond the northeastern limb in EUV imagery.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, setting the half-width to the swpc_cat tool's minimum 10 degrees. The CME is very narrow (narrower than 10 degrees half-width) so the speed could have been underestimated in this analysis.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs; analysis is approximate because of the faintness of the front
Best fit measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. CME is obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.
Updated measurement using additional SOHO LASCO C3 imagery. Measurement based on the source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Half-width may be less than 10 degrees. Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge becomes diffuse after a few frames, this measurement relies on early imagery as a result.

Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A.
Measurement based on best fit and source location.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The front of the CME becomes very diffuse as it progresses out in the C2/C3 and COR2A field of view.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulation performing from leading edge seen in SOHO C2 and STEREO A COR2 which determined with good confidence that the CME originates from the far-side
Leading edge measurement using SOHO C2 and STEREO A COR2 for triangulation out to about 8 solar radii.
Later measurement out to 8 solar radii which is much wider.
Measurement using best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCOC2, C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement using best fit between several SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 frames.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This CME has a small nose proceeding the main bulk that was not included in this measurement.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs confirms the far-sidedness of this CME, which is indicated by the opening of field lines seen behind the west limb in SDO AIA 171.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

A very approximate analysis based on the fit with 2 coronagraphs (which can also be obtained with significantly different longitudes). No source has been found in EUV imagery to corroborate this measurement. The measurement is very approximate because of the narrowness of this jet (it is definitely <10 deg halfwidth), its faintness and the fuzziness of its front, as well as because of its availability only in a few overlapping timestamps in each coronagraph.
Measurement using best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2, as well as source location seen in UV imagery.
Measurement of very diffuse leading edge using source location seen in UV imagery. Several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and a few frames of STEREO A COR2 were used to conduct this measurement.
Measurement of faint leading edge using source location seen in UV imagery and best fit between a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2.
Parameters based on best fit of features with source location longitude. There is likely deflection of the CME due to the coronal hole feature around the source location.
Measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and on source location visible on/near the E limb. This is a rough measurement due to difficulty in tracking the LE feature.
One-coronagraph (SOHO) analysis, based on the location of the likely source (a minor eruption in the NE unnumbered region to the NE of Active Region 14076.
Analysis based solely on the fit of this narrow CME with two coronagraphs. It tentatively confirms the source as the eruption in AR 4068, but with significant deflection to the SE.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Coronal signatures also indicate farsided source.

Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Due to the far northern latitude (~N85), there is a very wide range of possible longitudes. This is a very speculative longitude, and the CME could easily be farsided.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO C2 frames and a few frames of STEREO A COR2 out to about 7 solar radii.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO C2 and STEREO A COR2 to triangulate the source longitude out to 7 solar radii before the CME largely disappears from the field of view
Leading edge measurement using SOHO C2 and STEREO A COR2A to triangulate the leading edge; it appears that the CME emanates from farther beyond the western limb after measurement.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
Preliminary leading edge measurement with one spacecraft (STEREO A) assuming the known source is just beyond the western limb; to be confirmed with triangulation when SOHO imagery is available.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Parameters based on likely source location and best fit using STEREO A COR2 imagery. The longitude may vary by +/- 10 degrees due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints of source and CME.
Approximate analysis based solely on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO and STEREO A) as there is no candidate source in EUV imagery. The longitude could be different by +-5 degrees.
Approximate analysis based solely on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO and STEREO A), as no source has been found in EUV imagery.
Low confidence in velocity leading edge measurement using SOHO and STEREO A out to 8 solar radii; the leading edge is too diffuse to reliably measure much farther than this.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2). The front is very faint, especially in STEREO A COR2, so the longitude is somewhat uncertain.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit of the CME's very faint front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2).
Parameters based on best fit of features in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is some uncertainty due to faintness of event.
These parameters are based on best fit of feature using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The front is very diffuse and difficult to track which may have impacted the fit and these parameters.
Parameters based on source location and visible front in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation between SOHO C2 and STEREO A COR2 to determine a possible longitude, indicating with high confidence that the CME originates from the far-side.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation between SOHO C2 and STEREO A COR2 to determine a possible longitude, indicating with high confidence that the CME originates from the far-side.
Measurement based on source location beyond west limb as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Updated measurement with more available frames, triangulated between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A.
Triangulated measurement with SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A frames only, starting at 2025-05-06T07:06Z due to acceleration observed across all measurable frames.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO/STEREO A out to 12 solar radii reworked to ascertain a maximum latitude.
Updated measurement using additional SOHO LASCO C3 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 and C3). The measurement confirms the source (a minor filament eruption to the north of AR 14079) seen as post eruptive arcades.
Parameters based on best fit of brightest front in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Measured speeds ranged between 200 km/s to 350 km/s.
Leading edge measurement using two spacecraft to triangulate potential latitude/longitude.
Leading edge measurement assuming the source is right near the western limb using only one spacecraft (SOHO). The true CME half-width is less than 10 degrees.
One spacecraft leading edge measurement assuming that Active Region 4079 is the source of this eruption; the eruption may too be far-sided.
Two spacecraft measurement using SOHO/STEREO A to triangulate the longitude with medium confidence; probably not far-sided based on fit of the CME seen in one frame in COR2A.
Two spacecraft measurement using SOHO/STEREO A to triangulate the longitude with medium confidence; probably not far-sided based on fit of the CME seen in one frame in COR2A.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. Front is quite faint and diffuse, but has good confidence in the high southern latitude.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A.
Two spacecraft leading edge measurement, which determined the CME was likely far out of the ecliptic. Upper bound on half-width.

A very approximate analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). However, the front is so undefined/fuzzy except for 2 images (one in C2 and another in COR2A) that parameters are very uncertain. The source is likely far-sided.

A very uncertain analysis based on the assumption that activity in AR 4079 is the source of this CME and on the front in SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 coronagraph. The CME is covered by a data gap in STEREO A.

Very approximate analysis based on the likely source of this CME (minor filament eruption close to the southwest limb) and confirmed by the two coronagraph fit with the super faint front in STEREO A and with (a more easily traceable) front in SOHO LASCO C2.
Analysis based on the fit of the front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). There seems to be a northward deflection or rotation going on.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2) and somewhat confirmed by the possible coronal signature - faint dimming north of the disk center.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). The source is seen on/close to the NE limb.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of the faint fuzzy front in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2) and confirmed by the likely source location (eruption in a minor unnumbered region).
Very approximate analysis (+-10 degrees), based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2), taking into account the likely source - a filament off the east limb.
Analysis based solely on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2).
Very uncertain analysis based solely on the fit of the left boundary of this fuzzy faint CME in two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO).
A super approximate analysis based on fitting the halo in SOHO LASCO coronagraph. The CME front is not seen in STEREO A coronagraph at all. The source is likely far-sided.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement base don best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Two spacecraft measurement taking into account the faint and wide feature in STEREO Ahead out ahead of the brighter core portion in addition to the brightest portion of the bulk in SOHO C2/C3 which was required for triangulation to fit relative to the source of the eruption; assumes some eastward deflection due to a coronal hole located just northwest of the eruptive active region.
Two spacecraft measurement using SOHO and STEREO A with imagery that did not overlap due to an early SOHO data gap and relatively later STEREO A data gap; uncertainty in longitude about 15 degrees.

SOHO leading edge measurement out to 9 solar radii due to a STEREO A data gap; a SOHO plane of sky measurement with longitude 90 degrees west probably approximates to this eruption well to the nearest +/-5 degrees based on the filament eruption observed in SDO AIA/GOES SUVI 304.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This measurement relies on early imagery as this event is overtaken by CME: 2025-05-14T07:12Z after a few frames.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured parameters may vary slightly as the source is likely far-sided and a precise source location is unknown.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Only two frames of STEREO A COR2 are usable, so running difference image quality is low, but it is enough to track the front and triangulate.
Measurement based on the source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and one frame of STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulated measurement betweeen SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. The LASCO C3 pylon obscures CME visiblity in the running difference imagery and it cannot be resolved there.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There may be some uncertainty due to faintness of event.
Preliminary measurement based on source location and visible features in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. Uncertainty with parameters due to limited available frames and faintness of event.

This measurement is approximate due to lack of available triangulation. The only STEREO A COR2 frame in which this CME is visible is too faint to be usable for measurement. The source location is based on a visible on-disk source.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and on the visible source near the NE limb.
Measurement based on SOHO LASCO C2 only and on visible source location (AR 14090 S12E75). The frame in SOHO LASCO C3 in which this CME is visible is not clear enough to use in the measurement.

Very speculative analysis based on the fit of a very faint and uncertain front with two coronagraphs in one timestamp. There are very few images with the front of this CME seen in coronagraphs and its source is also very faint and speculative.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This CME is not measurable in SOHO LASCO C3 as it is blocked by the pylon.
Approximate analysis using best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
This analysis is based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2).
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO).
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2).
A very speculative analysis of this very faint event  without a found source. Analysis is based on the fit of the very faint and incomplete front in two coronagraphs, SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This CME appears to speed up in later frames.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Single spacecraft 3D measurement with SOHO LASCO C2 only. STEREO A COR2 not available due to a data gap, and the LASCO C3 pylon greatly obscures the CME front in white light and running difference imagery. As such, the latitude and longitude may vary +- 10 degrees in either direction, with good confidence in the southern deflection from the observed source via SDO AIA and GOES SUVI imagery.

Single spacecraft 3D measurement with SOHO LASCO C2 only, becoming notably more faint after several frames. STEREO A COR2 not available due to a data gap, and the LASCO C3 pylon greatly obscures the CME front in white light and running difference imagery. As such, the latitude and longitude may vary +- 10 degrees in either direction, with good confidence in the southern deflection from the observed source via SDO AIA and GOES SUVI imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Because the source is far-sided, a precise source location is unknown and the measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Parameters based on best fit in relation to source location. Parameters may vary by +/- 10 degrees due to overlapping features in coronagraph imagery.
Measurement is speculative. Imagery in LASCO C2 is only available in early frames, and LASCO C3 is only available in later frames. Imagery from STEREO A COR2 is very grainy.
Best fit measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and visible source location on/near the NE limb.
Best fit based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and visible source location.
Measurement based on the BULK of the CME. Parameters are based on best fit relying more heavily on STEREO A COR2 imagery to differentiate the bulk and shock features.

Measurement of faint SHOCK feature. Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. A portion of the shock front to the NW from SOHO LASCO C3's field-of-view was excluded from this fit due to measurement technique limitations.

Analysis based on the best fit of this narrow jet with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2) and confirmed by the location of the likely source (filament eruption). It looks like the CME is deflecting southward.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2) and supported by the range of possible longitudes (based on the CME appearing northeast in LASCO and northwest in STEREO A). This analysis is based on the clearer second front directly following a messier first front of the CME, as they appear in both coronagraphs.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.

Difficult triangulation with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, as STEREO A COR2 data quality and the diffuse front directed towards STEREO A made for tough visibility in running difference imagery. Triangulated north and west of the observed source region at S06W45, indicating some deflection.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. E90 is the triangulated longitude and this is not plane-of-sky. Due to the faint front and overlap with a concurrent streamer blowout, the earliest visible frames are omitted for a better tracking of the front.
Analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2) and confirming the source as the filament eruption significantly east of Active Region 14098.

A highly speculative measurement based on the location of the flare and the front in SOHO LASCO C2, adjusted for what is possibly the faint front in STEREO A COR2. The front in COR2 is extremely faint and is difficult to ascertain and the analysis is hence speculative.
Very approximate analysis based exclusively on the best fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2), since no source has been found in EUV imagery.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the location of the flare/eruption (AR 14100) and the best fit with two coronagraphs, SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2).
SHOCK measurement of the more faint outer feature, triangulated between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
BULK measurement of brighter inner feature, triangulated between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery.
Leading edge measurement encompassing the CME's path traversing the ecliptic plane as the CME pitch angle increases with time.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 for triangulation.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This event is too faint to be reliably tracked in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on visible source location.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and on visible source location in STEREO A EUVI.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This event is not measurable in SOHO LASCO C3 as it is obscured by the pylon.
Preliminary analysis of the SHOCK using about four frames of GOES CCOR-1 and four frames of SOHO LASCO C3.
Analysis of the BULK using about four frames of GOES CCOR-1, four frames of SOHO LASCO C3, and two frames of STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This CME is too faint to be reliably measured in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2. This CME was not visible to measure in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on best fit in STEREO A COR2 and low confidence fit in GOES CCOR. This CME does not reliably appear in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, as no clear source has been found.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

Best fit measurement using multiple frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and two usable frames of STEREO A COR2. Analysis of this CME was affected by a data gap in STEREO A COR2 which lasted from 2025-06-02T23:53Z to 2025-06-03T00:53Z, resulting in there being two usable frames before the tracked feature exited the STEREO A COR2 field of view.

Analysis of the first closed loop front (not the initial dimpled/broken/fuzzy front) based on the best fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). This analysis is approximate, as longitude of the fit can vary from -17 to 27 degrees.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of this narrow CME in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2). No source was found for this event, however it is worth noting that the CME latitude and longitude are just north of the recently opened coronal hole which was gradually 'opened' by a very slow motion eruption associated with the 2025-06-03T07:48Z CME.
Approximate analysis based on the fit of the fuzzy faint front in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2).
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The parameters may vary by +/- 10 degrees due to the faintness of the CME.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery.
Best fit measurement using triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 and source location seen in UV imagery of the disk. This CME was partially obstructed by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and one faint frame in STEREO A COR2 as well as visible source location over the NE limb.

Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 as well as visible source beyond the W limb. The couple of visible frames in COR2A help with triangulation, but the front itself is not defined enough to be used in the measurement.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Triangulated measurement between later frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 only, as the front fans out and widens, and deflects slightly northward, from its first visible frames in SOHO LASCO C2.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This CME is notably less than 10 half width and deflects northward, making it difficult to track with the swpc_cat tool. High confidence in its high northern latitude.
Best fit using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and a few frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, as well as source location seen in available UV imagery.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and STEREO A COR2 for two frames before the data gap begins at 2025-06-09T21:38Z. Measurement is at E90 and is not plane-of-sky.
Best fit using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and a few frames of STEREO A COR2, as well as source location seen in available UV imagery.
Measurement made using best triangulated fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured longitude may vary slightly.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured longitude may vary slightly as the source is far-sided and a precise source location is therefore unknown.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulated measurement with SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. CME appears slightly less wide in STEREO A COR2 imagery, LASCO C3 measured half-width is used here.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. CME appears to have deflected notably further north and west from the observed source near the limb (N16E90) via triangulation.

Triangulated measurement between later frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and two available, low quality running difference images of STEREO A COR2. The CME deflects northward as it travels through LASCO C2 to C3 so later frames capture the final width and direction better. STEREO A COR2 imagery becomes sparse over this CME leading to lower quality running difference imagery that is usable.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measured longitude based on the source location. A triangulated measurement was not available as this event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This CME overlaps the CME first seen in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2025-06-11T14:36Z.
Parameters are based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The leading edge is slightly diffuse which may contribute to some uncertainty with these parameters.

Triangulated measurement using several frames of GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. About half of this CME is obstructed by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. Analysis of this CME was affected by a data gap in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 which started at 2025-06-12T02:36Z and is scheduled to end at 2025-06-12T21:35Z.
Preliminary measurement. Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

Triangulated measurement using several frames of GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. Analysis of this CME was affected by a data gap in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 which started at 2025-06-12T02:36Z and is scheduled to end at 2025-06-12T21:35Z.

An extremely uncertain one-coronagraph (STEREO A COR2) analysis based on the location of the possible source of this CME, the filament eruption. The CME front of this CME is only seen in a few early timestamps of STEREO A COR2 coronagraph, superimposed on the previous CME. The CME is not seen in other coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the likely source of this CME (filament eruption in/near AR 14115) and confirmed by the good fit with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3).
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). The resulting latitude and longitude also loosely fit the likely source of the CME (filament eruption).
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO).
Approximate analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2), which seems to confirm the suspicion that Active Region 14115 might be the source of this event.
A very approximate analysis based on the fit of the fuzzy front in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2) and on the source - the filament northeast of Active Region 14111.
Approximate analysis based on the fit of the narrow front in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2), confirming the source as likely being behind the west limb.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The CME becomes too faint/diffuse to track far out into the field of view.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Preliminary measurement using GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT. The latitude and longitude are based on the best fit in imagery and estimated due to the eruption's deflection to the northwest.

Approximate analysis encompassing this whole event (which possibly consists of two consecutive CMEs) - encompassing its eastern earlier front and the later, more western (possibly slower) front. Analysis is heavily based on the longitude of the likely source (flare and eruption near Active Region 14114) and on the fits with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). The fit indicates there was a very significant deflection of the CME northward.

Very approximate analysis based on the fitting of the front that is super faint in STEREO A COR2 but is clear in early timestamps of SOHO LASCO C2 with two coronagraphs. The resulting longitude and latitude correspond well to the location of the Active Region 4105, which is a likely source.

Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2) and loosely confirmed by the source location of the flare, Active Region 4105. It looks like there is some deflection northeastwards.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of the front in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2) and confirmed by a faint dimming to the east of the flaring active region 14105 seen mostly in EUVI A 195.

Measurement using only SOHO LASCO C2 frames since the leading edge becomes diffuse before reaching the edge of the C2 field of view. Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and the location of the source at W90 as identified in available UV imagery.
Best fit measurement using multiple frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery, as well as the location of the on-disk source and likely deflection observed in available UV imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. There was no SOHO LASCO imagery available during this analysis.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is a portion of the irregularly shaped front that was excluded from this fit.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of the front of this CME in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). The source is on the limb or closely behind it so the longitude could be somewhat different.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 as well as visible source.
Triangulated with SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 as well as visible source location.

Triangulated measurement between early frames SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 before it comes too faint to resolve well. Longitude could vary W90-W110 based on fitting attempts, uncertainty due to faint front.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 as well as visible source location.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement made using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery, as well as location of source seen in available UV imagery. Leading edge becomes diffuse abut halfway through the STEREO A COR2 field of view.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, as well as location of source seen in available UV imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Parameters are based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Updated measurement using additional later frames in all coronagraphs. With the backfill of SOHO imagery, the updated start time of this CME could be 2025-06-18T23:36Z as seen in SOHO LASCO C2.
Parameters based on best fit of the BULK of the CME using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Leading edge measurement of the CME taken as a whole using STEREO A/SOHO for triangulation.
Best fit measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. Analysis of this CME was affected by a campaign in STEREO A COR2.
Best fit measurement using several frames of GOES CCOR-1 and a few frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement using best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 as well as visible source location.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on low confidence triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

High uncertainty single spacecraft measurement based on imagery from SOHO LASCO C3 and visible source location. Two frames from STEREO A COR2 aided with fit parameters, but the imagery quality was too grainy to be included in the measurement.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, front is notably faint and difficult to track.
Single spacecraft measurement based on visible deflection in source signature.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Very approximate analysis based solely on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1), as there is no clear source on disk.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2. STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Approximate analysis based on the fit of this narrow jet with three coronagraphs. There is no clear source for this CME in EUV imagery to confirm these parameters.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. The front of this event was too diffuse to reliably track in SOHO LASCO C2.
Parameters are based on best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Real-time SOHO imagery only had a few frames available which are not used in this fit.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Best fit measurement using STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
An extremely approximate analysis based on the fit of a very faint, indistinct front in very few timestamps of two coronagraphs (C2 and STEREO A COR2).
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This is a lower confidence measurement due to limited usable imagery.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Approximate analysis based on the fit of the front in two coronagraphs (GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2)
Preliminary analysis based on the fit of the front in three coronagraphs.
Best fit measurement triangulating between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

Best fit measurement using several frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. One frame of SOHO LASCO C3 used. Some uncertainty due to the leading edge feature tracked being very faint. This CME was partially obstructed by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.
Parameters based on best fit and possible source location.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Parameters based on best fit in STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO C3, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on potential source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Not clearly measurable with GOES CCOR-1. The CME was only measurable in a few frames of running difference imagery before the leading edge became too faint to measure.
Triangulation with SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery only, as they best observe the faint front with available imagery.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. CME not measured in SOHO LASCO C3 because it is obscured by the pylon.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT, with potential deflection from the source location.
Approximate analysis mostly based on the fit with three coronagraphs, but also taking into account that the filament is likely erupting from behind the west limb as seen in SDO AIA.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs. The source is not very clear.
Approximate analysis based on the location of the erupting filament (N35W90), as seen from STEREO A and on the fit with all three coronagraphs.
An extremely approximate analysis based on the fit of the very faint, difficult to trace front in all three coronagraphs. The source is unclear.
